included_relationships.notes: Leave is taken by the employee (self) in connection with their adoption of a child. Child includes biological, adopted, foster, stepchild, legal ward, child of employee or employee's domestic partner, or person for whom employee stands in loco parentis per 2 CCR § 11087(c).

conditions.source_text: placement of a child with an employee in connection with the adoption or foster care of the child by the employee

relationship_issues: included_relationships.required_value lists only 'child', but the regulation chunk 2 CCR § 11087(j)(1) grants leave for placement of a child with the employee in connection with adoption by the employee — the covered relationship is the employee themselves (self) adopting a child, not a family member. 'self' should be included as the primary relationship since the employee is the one adopting.

The child relationship sub-types listed in the nested condition (biological, adopted, foster, stepchild, legal ward, child of domestic partner, in loco parentis) belong in included_relationships per 2 CCR § 11087(c), not as a nested sub-check inside conditions.
logic_issues: The nested 'child relationship' check inside placement_for_adoption duplicates what belongs in included_relationships and should be removed from conditions.

The nested 'time since placement' check has null operator and null required_value — this is an invalid/incomplete check and should be removed since no time-since-placement deadline is stated in the provided regulation chunks.
The 'adoption connection' check required_value is a long paraphrase of the regulation rather than a short atomic value.
unsupported_conditions: The nested 'child relationship' sub-check inside placement_for_adoption is not a qualifying condition for the leave reason itself — it belongs only in included_relationships.
The 'time since placement' nested check has no support in the provided regulation chunks and should be removed entirely.
format_issues: Nested check object 'time since placement' has null operator and null required_value, which is invalid per the output structure rules.
The 'adoption connection' required_value is a full regulatory sentence rather than a short atomic phrase.
revision_summary: Added 'self' to included_relationships.required_value since the employee is the one adopting the child per 2 CCR § 11087(j)(1).
Kept 'child' in included_relationships.required_value and moved child sub-type definitions to notes, grounded in 2 CCR § 11087(c).
Removed the invalid 'time since placement' nested check (null operator, null value, no regulatory support).
Removed the 'child relationship' nested check from conditions since relationship coverage belongs only in included_relationships.
Simplified 'adoption connection' required_value to a short atomic phrase.
Resulting condition is a clean ALL_OF with two supported sub-checks: placement with employee and adoption connection.
